children.text: the regulations and procedures used by the entities referred to in paragraph (1) to apply and enforce such laws to themselves and their employees.
Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ( et seq.), and related provisions of .42 U.S.C. 2000esection 2302 of title 5
The Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 ( et seq.), and related provisions of .29 U.S.C. 621section 2302 of title 5
The Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 ( et seq.), and related provisions of .42 U.S.C. 12101section 2302 of title 5
The Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 ( et seq.), and related provisions of sections 6381 through 6387 of title 5.29 U.S.C. 2611
The Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 ( et seq.), and related provisions of sections 5541 through 5550a of title 5.29 U.S.C. 201
The Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 ( et seq.), and related provisions of .29 U.S.C. 651section 7902 of title 5
The Rehabilitation Act of 1973 ( et seq.).29 U.S.C. 701
Chapter 71 (relating to Federal service labor-management relations) of title 5.
The General Accounting Office Personnel Act of 1980 ( et seq.).31 U.S.C. 731
The Employee Polygraph Protection Act of 1988 ( et seq.).29 U.S.C. 2001
The Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification Act ( et seq.).29 U.S.C. 2101
Chapter 43 (relating to veterans’ employment and reemployment) of title 38.

children.heading: Contents of study and recommendations
children.label: c Contents of study and recommendations
children.text: The study under this section shall evaluate whether the rights, protections, and procedures, including administrative and judicial relief, applicable to the entities listed in paragraph (1) of subsection (a) and their employees are comprehensive and effective and shall include recommendations for any improvements in regulations or legislation, including proposed regulatory or legislative language.

children.heading: Deadline and delivery of study
children.label: d Deadline and delivery of study

children.text: the Board shall prepare and complete the study and recommendations required under this section; and

the Board shall transmit such study and recommendations (with the Board’s comments) to the head of each entity considered in the study, and to the Congress by delivery to the Speaker of the House of Representatives and President pro tempore of the Senate for referral to the appropriate committees of the House of Representatives and of the Senate.
